sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Lunar New Year film 'Scare Out' sells $14.5M in tickets before debut
feed.summary: The Generation Essentials Group (TGE) announced that its Lunar New Year film, "Scare Out," has achieved over US$14.5 million in pre-sales, placing it among the top three holiday box-office forecasts. The movie's promotional campaigns generated significant social media engagement, with 5.40 billion total views and 2.72 billion views for its Douyin hashtag, ranking #1 on Weibo's movie list. "Scare Out" is set for a broad international release in major markets, including Greater China, North America, the UK, and Australia.

feed.title: Cytokinetics Announces European Commission Approval of MYQORZO® (aficamten) for the Treatment of Adults with Symptomatic Obstructive Hypertrophic Cardiomyopathy
feed.summary: Cytokinetics announced that the European Commission (EC) has approved MYQORZO® (aficamten) for the treatment of symptomatic obstructive hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (oHCM) in adult patients. This approval is based on positive results from the SEQUOIA-HCM clinical trial, demonstrating significant improvements in exercise capacity and symptoms. The company plans its first European launch in Germany in Q2 2026, offering a new treatment option for patients with oHCM.

feed.title: New MYQORZO drug wins EU nod to treat symptomatic obstructive HCM
feed.summary: Cytokinetics has received European Commission approval for MYQORZO (aficamten) tablets to treat symptomatic obstructive hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (NYHA class II-III) in adults. The first European launch is planned for Germany in Q2 2026, with flexible dosing options. This approval follows prior FDA and China NMPA approvals, broadening the drug's global reach, and is based on positive results from the SEQUOIA-HCM Phase 3 clinical trial.

feed.title: |【即時新聞】富蘭克林高收益債ETF宣布最新月配息，每股將發放0.131美元現金股利
feed.summary: |Franklin High Yield Corporate ETF (FLHY) has announced a monthly dividend of $0.131 per share. The ex-dividend and record dates are both February 2nd, with the payable date set for February 5th. This continues the ETF's policy of stable monthly cash distributions, allowing investors to plan their cash flow accordingly.

feed.title: Patrick Dumont Appointed CEO and Chairman of Las Vegas Sands Corp
feed.summary: Patrick Dumont has been appointed as the new Chairman and CEO of Las Vegas Sands Corp., effective March 1, 2026, succeeding Robert G. Goldstein. Dumont, with over 25 years of experience and a history with Sands since 2010, has been instrumental in the company's growth strategy, including an $8 billion project in Singapore. His leadership is expected to drive the company's expansion in key markets, focusing on innovation, customer-centric development, and sustainability.

feed.title: CNH: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: CNH Industrial NV reported a strong fourth quarter, with profit of $86 million and earnings of 19 cents per share, surpassing Wall Street's expectations of 11 cents per share. The truck, tractor, and bus manufacturer also exceeded revenue forecasts, posting $5.16 billion against an expected $5.02 billion. For the full year, CNH reported a profit of $510 million on revenue of $18.1 billion, and projects full-year earnings between 35 and 45 cents per share.

feed.title: Medtronic: Fiscal Q3 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Medtronic PLC reported fiscal third-quarter earnings of $1.14 billion, or 89 cents per share, which adjusted to $1.36 per share, exceeding Wall Street expectations. The medical device company also surpassed revenue forecasts with $9.02 billion and anticipates full-year earnings between $5.62 and $5.66 per share. This financial performance is based on data from Zacks Investment Research.

feed.title: Genuine Parts: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Genuine Parts Co. (GPC) reported a significant loss of $609.5 million in its fourth quarter, translating to $4.39 per share, missing Wall Street expectations. Adjusted earnings were $1.55 per share, below the average analyst estimate of $1.79. The company's revenue of $6.01 billion also fell short of forecasts, and it projects full-year earnings between $7.50 and $8 per share.

feed.title: Leidos (LDOS) Surpasses Q4 EPS Expectations, Misses on Revenue
feed.summary: Leidos Holdings, Inc. (LDOS) reported Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.76, exceeding estimates by $0.15, but missed on revenue with $4.2 billion, a 4% decline attributed to an extra work week in the prior fiscal year and a government shutdown. Despite the quarterly revenue dip, annual revenue increased by 3% to $17.17 billion, driven by demand in air defense and cyber sectors. The company projects fiscal 2026 revenues between $17.5 billion and $17.9 billion, maintaining a strong financial health evidenced by an Altman Z-Score of 3.97 and a Piotroski F-Score of 8.

feed.title: Gartner (IT) Receives Underperform Rating with $150 Price Target from BofA
feed.summary: Bank of America has initiated coverage on Gartner (IT) with an Underperform rating and a $150 price target, despite an overall optimistic outlook for the Information and Business Services sector. Gartner's financial health shows strong revenue growth and profitability, but also high leverage and sector-specific risks. Investors should consider these mixed signals when evaluating the company's potential.

feed.title: InterDigital (IDCC) Secures New Patent License Agreement with Sony
feed.summary: InterDigital (IDCC) has renewed its patent license agreement with Sony (SONY), expanding its reach in wireless, video, and AI technologies. Despite this positive development, both companies saw a decline in premarket share prices. While InterDigital demonstrates strong financial health and profitability, robust growth, and positive analyst sentiment, its valuation metrics are nearing historical highs, and there's recent insider selling activity, suggesting caution for potential investors.

feed.title: CoStar Group (CSGP) Receives Neutral Rating and $52 Price Target from BofA
feed.summary: Bank of America has resumed coverage of CoStar Group (CSGP) with a Neutral rating and a $52 price target, despite the company's strong position as a leader in commercial real estate data. While CoStar boasts a high gross margin and robust financial stability with subscription-based revenue, it faces challenges including declining operating margins and earnings growth. The stock's valuation metrics show a mixed sentiment, with a very high P/E ratio but P/S and P/B ratios near 10-year lows, alongside significant institutional ownership and insider selling activity.

feed.title: Genuine Parts Company Plans Automotive-Industrial Business Split
feed.summary: Genuine Parts Company (GPC) announced plans to separate its automotive (NAPA brand) and industrial (Motion brand) divisions into two independent, publicly traded companies. This tax-free transaction is targeted for completion in the first quarter of 2027 and aims to create two focused entities: Global Automotive and Global Industrial. The split is expected to sharpen customer alignment, simplify operations, and enable disciplined, business-specific investments for long-term value.

feed.title: Vulcan Materials Company Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results
feed.summary: Vulcan Materials Company announced strong financial results for the fourth quarter and full year 2025, driven by increased revenues, earnings, and operating cash flow. The company saw growth in its aggregates segment due to healthy public construction activity and effective cost management. Vulcan also provided a positive outlook for 2026, anticipating continued strength in public construction and improved private nonresidential opportunities.

feed.title: Fluor sells 71 million NuScale Power shares for $1.35 billion
feed.summary: Fluor Corporation announced it successfully sold 71 million shares of NuScale Power for $1.35 billion and is working to monetize its remaining 40 million shares by Q2 2026. Including previous sales, Fluor has generated nearly $2 billion from its NuScale Power holdings. The company also repurchased almost 17 million of its own shares for over $700 million and increased its share repurchase program, with further details expected during its fourth-quarter earnings call.

feed.title: CNH Industrial Releases 2026 Outlook with Q4 Earnings Beat
feed.summary: CNH Industrial reported a strong Q4 performance, exceeding EPS and revenue expectations. The company provided its 2026 outlook, projecting a slight decline to flat net sales for its agriculture segment, partly due to currency effects. JPMorgan downgraded CNH Industrial, citing a potential downside for North America's large agriculture equipment sales beyond 2025.
